betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Lathe§f is a 2×4×2 multiblock machine from libVulpes that shapes raw ingots and plates into §6rods§f and §6wires§f for downstream AR circuits and motors. Its structure (TileLathe.java:23) is a hollow box: §6Structure Blocks§f form the four walls, a §6Motor§f sits on the top-back layer, an §6Input Hatch§f ('I') faces the front, an §6Output Hatch§f ('O') faces the back, a §6Power Plug§f ('P') and the §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3) complete the shell.

§6Production Path (libVulpes libvulpes + TileLathe.java)§f:
- Power: 30 RF/t while running (libVulpes default)
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: 1x §6Iron Rod§f (libvulpes:productrod:1), 1x §6Item IO Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:4), 1x §6Machine Structure§f (libvulpes:structuremachine), 1x §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3), 1x §6Steel Plate§f (libvulpes:productplate:6), 1x §6Steel Gear§f (libvulpes:productgear:6)
- Recipes are added at runtime through `RecipesMachine.getInstance().addRecipe(new RecipeLathe(...))` (RecipeLathe.java:6) and listed in JEI under category §6Advanced Rocketry: Lathe§f

The lathe is a prerequisite for almost every mid-tier machine in the AR progression because rods are required by Precision Assemblers, Centrifuges, and most rocket components. The structure is too small to need glass windows; just place the controller, then build the frame with structure blocks and cap the top with a motor and 'I' hatch.

§eTip§f: The control circuit board (ic:3) doubles as the multiblock marker — right-click it with a §6Wrench§f to assemble the preview. It is required for §6Precision Assembler§f (10012) and §6Centrifuge§f (10018).
